intro.title: The Three Battles East
intro.body: Alexander crossed the Hellespont in May 334 BCE with ~40,000 men. The three set-piece battles that broke the Achaemenid empire — Granicus, Issus, Gaugamela — span three campaigning seasons. The route shown is the strategic arc; the actual march was longer and more sinuous.

stops.name: Hellespont crossing
stops.note: Alexander throws his spear into Asian soil from the prow (Arrian I.11). The army crosses by relay near Abydos.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Narrow strait crowded with low transports, royal trireme leading, two coasts visible.

stops.name: Granicus
stops.note: First major battle, May 334 BCE. Alexander's cavalry charge across the river breaks the Persian line (Arrian I.13-16).
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. River-crossing cavalry battle, Companion cavalry breasting the bank, Persian satraps in plumed helmets.

stops.name: Issus
stops.note: November 333 BCE. Darius III chooses ground at the Pinarus river; Alexander's right-flank thrust panics him (Arrian II.8-11).
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Narrow coastal plain between mountains and sea, sarissa phalanx versus Persian center, Darius's chariot turning.

stops.name: Tyre
stops.note: Seven-month siege, January-July 332 BCE. Alexander builds a mole from the mainland to the island (Arrian II.16-24).
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Island fortress under siege, a stone causeway extending from the mainland, siege towers on rollers.

stops.name: Gaugamela
stops.note: 1 October 331 BCE. Alexander declines Darius's offer to share the empire and wins the decisive battle east of the Tigris (Arrian III.8-15).
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Vast plain with two opposed armies, scythed chariots, dust, Alexander's oblique advance angling rightward.
